title: The Peacock and the Crane
lines: A Peacock, proud of his beautiful tail, mocked a Crane for having such dull feathers.
"Look at my glorious tail," said the Peacock.
"It is true," replied the Crane, "that my feathers are not bright and glorious like yours."
"But see, I can fly high in the sky, while you must keep to the ground."
"Your beautiful tail may dazzle the eye, but it cannot lift you from the earth."
moral: True worth is not in appearance but in ability.
